subject: DevOps
title: Imperative: Script the Steps
description: A short lesson to help you apply Imperative: Script the Steps.

hook.text: You write a script: 'Create VPC. Create subnet A. Create subnet B. Attach load balancer.' Step by step.

buildup.text: Imperative IaC tells the tool exactly what to do. Bash scripts, Ansible playbooks, and AWS CLI commands are imperative.

discovery.text: The script doesn't know what already exists. Run it twice and it might try to create duplicate resources.

twist.text: You need to add checks: 'If VPC exists, skip. If not, create.' The complexity grows fast with every resource.

climax.text: Imperative works for one-off tasks and bootstrapping. For ongoing management, declarative is almost always better.

punchline.text: Imperative says how. Declarative says what. Declarative scales better.

quiz.question: What is a key disadvantage of imperative IaC scripts?
options: They don't track current state and may create duplicate resources
They require a graphical user interface
